essence: Identifies, measures, and controls workplace hazards to protect employee health and prevent long‑term exposure risks.

identity.title: Occupational Hygienist
identity.summary: Assesses chemical, biological, physical, and ergonomic hazards in workplaces, recommending controls to protect employee health and ensure regulatory compliance.

salary.notes: Specialists in high‑risk industries (oil & gas, pharmaceuticals, heavy manufacturing) can exceed £75k-£95k+.

qualifications: Training in occupational hygiene or environmental health
Knowledge of exposure monitoring and hazard assessment
Ability to measure noise, dust, chemicals, and other risks
Understanding of workplace legislation and control strategies

lifestyle.description: Occupational hygienists work across factories, laboratories, construction sites, and industrial facilities. They measure exposures, analyse risks, and recommend controls to protect employee health. The role suits individuals who enjoy scientific investigation, data analysis, and improving workplace safety.

habits: Analyses data and identifies health risks
Communicates findings to managers and employees
